macro_events.event_summary: Trump escalates rhetoric on Iran ("clock is ticking"), raising geopolitical risk premium on oil ahead of Nvidia earnings, with markets focused on crude prices and Treasury yields.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from potential Iran supply disruption / Strait of Hormuz risk
Defense spending tailwind from heightened Middle East tensions
Risk-off flow into safe havens (gold, Treasuries) competing with rate concerns

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with upstream leverage to higher Brent/WTI; ~40% of earnings tied to upstream realizations, direct beneficiary of geopolitical risk premium.
Major integrated with significant upstream exposure; Permian and international production gains pricing leverage on any Iran-driven crude spike.
Permian pure-play with highest beta to WTI among large caps; every $1 WTI move ~$200M FCF sensitivity.
Prime contractor for missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) directly relevant to Israel/Gulf state demand if Iran conflict escalates; recent precedent of replenishment orders after Mideast strikes.
Patriot missile and Tomahawk manufacturer; direct beneficiary of any kinetic action against Iran via munitions replenishment contracts.
Product tanker rates spike on Strait of Hormuz disruption risk as ~20% of global oil transits there; Scorpio is largest pure-play product tanker.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of operating cost; Delta has historically had less hedging than peers, direct margin hit from crude spike.
Similar jet fuel exposure with significant international/transatlantic routes that could see demand softness if Mideast conflict broadens.
Domestic-only carrier with no fuel hedges remaining; pure cost-side exposure to crude move.
Cruise operator with high bunker fuel costs and discretionary travel demand sensitivity if geopolitical anxiety rises.
Earnings tonight into a tape distracted by oil/yields; higher yields compress multiples on long-duration tech regardless of print quality, raising bar for post-earnings reaction.

headlines: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices, Yields In Focus As Trump Says 'Clock Is Ticking' For Iran; Nvidia Earnings Ahead
